stem: An engineer takes 20 measurements of room temperature using a thermometer. The measurements have a mean of 171.8 °C and a standard deviation of 17.5 °C. However, calibration tests reveal that a thermometer systematically overestimates the true value by between 2.3% and 7.0%. Estimate the true room temperature and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 171.8 °C. After correcting for the 4.7% bias: ≈ 164.1 °C. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.3%-7.0%) and sampling variability: [152.8, 175.7]. Point estimate: 164.1 °C.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
